title: SGLT2 Inhibitors and Heart Failure
summary: Clinical meta-analysis example normalized into the canonical E156 bundle schema with completed multi-persona review gating.
primary_estimand: Hazard ratio for heart failure hospitalisation or cardiovascular death
data: 9 RCTs, 71,553 patients, MEDLINE and Cochrane CENTRAL (through January 2026)
body: Do SGLT2 inhibitors reduce heart failure hospitalisation and cardiovascular death in patients across the full spectrum of ejection fraction? We included 9 randomised controlled trials enrolling 71,553 patients with heart failure or high cardiovascular risk, retrieved from MEDLINE and Cochrane CENTRAL through January 2026. Random-effects meta-analysis was performed on the log-hazard-ratio scale using restricted maximum-likelihood estimation of between-trial variance with the Hartung-Knapp correction applied to confidence intervals. SGLT2 inhibitors reduced the composite of heart failure hospitalisation or cardiovascular death by 23% (HR 0.77, 95% CI 0.72-0.83, I2 18%), with consistent benefit across reduced and preserved ejection fraction subgroups. The result was robust to leave-one-out sensitivity analysis, trim-and-fill adjustment for small-study effects, and restriction to trials with active comparators. SGLT2 inhibitors confer a clinically meaningful, statistically robust reduction in heart failure events, supporting their use irrespective of ejection fraction category. Indirect comparisons across drug subclasses were not feasible; differential effects of empagliflozin versus dapagliflozin cannot be excluded.

checks.detail: Body must not contain blank-line paragraph breaks.

checks.detail: Sentence 4 should include a confidence interval.

checks.name: result sentence has estimand

checks.name: boundary sentence present
checks.detail: Sentence 7 should express a limitation, harm, or scope boundary.

primary_plot.caption: No study-level numeric rows are embedded in this normalized example bundle.

clinician.notes: The treatment contrast, composite endpoint, and bedside implication are clear enough for a clinician reading the body alone.

statistician.notes: The pooled hazard ratio, confidence interval, and heterogeneity term are stated directly, and the interpretation stays within the reported composite outcome.

methods_editor.notes: All seven sentence roles are preserved, with the subgroup note in sentence four and the subclass caution kept in the boundary sentence.

skeptic.notes: The paragraph argues for benefit but does not claim head-to-head subclass superiority, and it leaves room for residual uncertainty across drug subclasses.

reader.notes: The sequence reads smoothly and remains screenshot-readable despite the dense method and robustness clauses.

review.body: Do SGLT2 inhibitors reduce heart failure hospitalisation and cardiovascular death in patients across the full spectrum of ejection fraction? We included 9 randomised controlled trials enrolling 71,553 patients with heart failure or high cardiovascular risk, retrieved from MEDLINE and Cochrane CENTRAL through January 2026. Random-effects meta-analysis was performed on the log-hazard-ratio scale using restricted maximum-likelihood estimation of between-trial variance with the Hartung-Knapp correction applied to confidence intervals. SGLT2 inhibitors confer a clinically meaningful, statistically robust reduction in heart failure events, supporting their use irrespective of ejection fraction category. Indirect comparisons across drug subclasses were not feasible; differential effects of empagliflozin versus dapagliflozin cannot be excluded.
